name: bully

documentation.readme: Bully is a new implementation of the WPS brute force attack, written in C. It is conceptually identical to other programs, in that it exploits the (now well known) design flaw in the WPS specification. It has several advantages over the original reaver code. These include fewer dependencies, improved memory and cpu performance, correct handling of endianness, and a more robust set of options. It runs on Linux, and was specifically developed to run on embedded Linux systems (OpenWrt, etc) regardless of architecture.

arguments.name: -A
arguments.description: : Disable ACK check for sent packets [No]

arguments.name: -B
arguments.description: : Bruteforce the WPS pin checksum digit [No]

arguments.name: --bruteforce
arguments.description: : Bruteforce the WPS pin checksum digit [No]

arguments.name: --detectlock
arguments.description: : Detect WPS lockouts unreported by AP [No]

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: : Force continue in spite of warnings [No]

arguments.name: -L
arguments.description: : Ignore WPS locks reported by the AP [No]

arguments.name: -l
arguments.description: : Seconds to wait if the AP locks WPS [43]

arguments.name: --lockignore
arguments.description: : Ignore WPS locks reported by the AP [No]

arguments.name: --lockwait
arguments.description: : Seconds to wait if the AP locks WPS [43]

arguments.name: --noacks
arguments.description: : Disable ACK check for sent packets [No]

arguments.name: --nofcs
arguments.description: : Packets don't contain the FCS field [Auto]

arguments.name: -P
arguments.description: : Use probe request for nonbeaconing AP [No]

arguments.name: --pin1delay
arguments.description: : Delay M seconds every Nth nack at M5 [0,1]

arguments.name: --pin2delay
arguments.description: : Delay M seconds every Nth nack at M7 [5,1]

arguments.name: --probe
arguments.description: : Use probe request for nonbeaconing AP [No]

arguments.name: -r
arguments.description: : Resend packets N times when not acked [2]

arguments.name: --retries
arguments.description: : Resend packets N times when not acked [2]

arguments.name: -T
arguments.description: : Test mode (do not inject any packets) [No]

arguments.name: --test
arguments.description: : Test mode (do not inject any packets) [No]

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

function_signatures.name: scan
function_signatures.description: Perform a read-only scan / probe of the target.

function_signatures.name: exploit
function_signatures.description: Run the offensive step against the target. Returns ok, data, error envelope.

function_signatures.name: report
function_signatures.description: Render the result into a human-readable or machine-readable report.

function_signatures.name: validate
function_signatures.description: Pre-flight check: confirm the target is reachable and the tool can run.

function_signatures.name: cleanup
function_signatures.description: Remove any artifacts left behind by the tool (pcaps, sessions, files).

function_signatures.name: chain
function_signatures.description: Take a previous tool's result and feed it as input to this tool.

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd bully*/`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd src/`; `usage: bully <options> interface`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [wifi_2_4_ghz, wifi_5_ghz, shell_linux] in the Wireless category.
